sprint: Fix real user-reported clone failure -- 'overlay' missing from LAYER_TARGETS
task: A real 'My Artwork Gallery' clone attempt failed Validation and fell back to a local draft. Reproduced with the real production template (not a fixture): authored a real Artwork Gallery project with a Thumbnail, a Frame-assigned Place, and a hosted Text Experience, built it for real, then drove the actual clone UI flow. Real error: Layer target 'overlay' rejected as invalid. Root cause: the Builder Convergence Sprint added target:'overlay' as a genuine 5th containership scope months ago -- builder.js's own convergeSceneLayer() emits it and the renderer paints it correctly -- but constants.js's LAYER_TARGETS (the validator's own accepted-value list) was never updated to include it. Stayed invisible because the original author's own Build validates BEFORE convergence runs, so an overlay-target Layer is only ever born after the one check that would reject it; the Scene Synthesis clone feature (previous entry) is the first path that round-trips compiled output back through Validation, surfacing the gap. Fixed in both tools/world-builder-v2 and tools/world-builder's constants.js (identical convergeScenes()/overlay logic in both). Deliberately scoped to the validator's allow-list only -- the Layer Pack editor's own UI dropdown still offers only the original four, since overlay is never meant to be hand-authored. Verified: the exact real reproduction now auto-Builds, auto-Validates clean, and auto-Publishes successfully. Full regression across 14 scripts + a new reproduction test + both goldenBuild.js suites (v1 and v2) unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
